211.title: Ravnopravnost i prava — rječnik rasprave
211.intro: Equality is one of ASOO's most demanding B1 speaking blocks: the topic is sensitive, so the words must be precise. Learn these twelve nouns inside real collocations — the phrase they live in matters as much as the word itself.

items.note: Neuter plural: ljudska prava = human rights; jednaka prava za sve. G prava. Singular pravo = a right / the law.
Feminine (i-declension). jednakost pred zakonom = equality before the law. G jednakosti.
ravnopravnost spolova = gender equality. The everyday word for 'being on equal footing'.
diskriminacija na temelju spola, dobi ili podrijetla = discrimination on the basis of sex, age or origin.
poštovati nekoga = to respect someone; s poštovanjem = respectfully (letter close). uz dužno poštovanje = with due respect.
jednake prilike = equal opportunities; dobiti priliku = to get a chance. Also 'occasion': u ovoj prilici.
jednake mogućnosti za sve; imati mogućnost + G/infinitive. Feminine i-declension, G mogućnosti.
ravnopravnost spolova (G pl spolova); bez obzira na spol = regardless of gender.
Feminine i-declension: G/I dobi. diskriminacija na temelju dobi. Note: godine = years, dob = age as a category.
nacionalna manjina = national minority; prava manjina. Opposite: većina = majority.
imati predrasude prema nekome = to hold prejudices toward someone (prema + dative). Usually used in the plural.
pristup obrazovanju i zdravstvu = access to education and healthcare (+ dative). Also 'an approach': nov pristup problemu.

211.title: Kako se ne slagati pristojno — ograde
211.intro: The highest register of B1 speech is disagreeing without offending. These hedges soften an opinion, buy you thinking time and signal respect for the other side. On a sensitive topic they are worth more than any single argument.

items.note: Softer than 'Mislim da'. Dative experiencer + da-clause: Čini mi se da nismo posve ravnopravni.
Conditional as a hedge. Male speaker: Rekao bih; female: Rekla bih.
Opens space for the other person before you state your view. griješiti = to be mistaken.

items.hr: Ne bih se u potpunosti složio.
items.note: Polite partial disagreement. Male: složio; female: složila. složiti se s + instrumental.

items.hr: S jedne strane…, s druge strane…
items.en: On one hand…, on the other hand…
items.note: The classic balancing frame that shows you see both sides.
ovisiti o + locative: Ovisi o situaciji. A neutral way to avoid a flat answer.
imati pravo = to be right (not 'biti u pravu' only). donekle = to some extent.
Also: po meni. Followed by a full clause: Po mojem mišljenju, to nije pravedno.
A gentle way to reject a claim. Male: rekao; female: rekla.

items.en: I agree to a certain extent.
items.note: Signals qualified agreement before you add a 'but'. do neke mjere = to some degree.

211.title: Ravnopravnost u praksi
211.intro: Deploy the equality vocabulary in real case forms and pick the polite hedge over the blunt one. Difficulty climbs from a single word to a preposition-plus-case trap.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'equality'?
questions.explanation: jednakost = equality (abstract noun in -ost). jednako = equally (adverb); jedan = one.

questions.prompt: 'human rights' — ljudska ______.
questions.explanation: prava = rights (neuter plural). pravda = justice; pravila = rules. ljudska prava is fixed.

questions.prompt: 'gender equality' — ravnopravnost ______. (spol → G pl)
questions.explanation: ravnopravnost governs the genitive: ravnopravnost spolova. spol → spolova (genitive plural).

questions.prompt: Which is the polite way to disagree?
options: Ne bih se u potpunosti složio, ali razumijem te.
questions.answer: Ne bih se u potpunosti složio, ali razumijem te.
questions.explanation: The conditional hedge softens the disagreement; the other three are rude and cost you register points.

questions.prompt: Hedge: 'It seems to me that…' — ______ mi se da imamo jednake prilike. (činiti → 3rd sg)
questions.explanation: Dative experiencer frame: Čini mi se da… = It seems to me that… The subject is the da-clause, so the verb is 3rd sg.

questions.prompt: 'It depends on the situation.' — Ovisi o ______.
questions.explanation: ovisiti o + locative: o situaciji. The accusative situaciju and instrumental situacijom would be wrong here.

questions.prompt: Build the balancing hedge: 'On one hand you are right.'
questions.explanation: s jedne strane = on one hand (s + genitive jedne strane); imati pravo = to be right.

questions.prompt: 'discrimination on the basis of age' — diskriminacija na temelju ______. (dob)
questions.explanation: na temelju + genitive; dob is a feminine i-declension noun, so genitive = dobi.

questions.prompt: 'prejudices toward minorities' — predrasude ______ manjinama.
questions.explanation: predrasude prema + dative: prema manjinama. prema governs the dative and means 'toward'.

211.title: Rasprava o ravnopravnosti na poslu
211.intro: Your speaking session: you and your wife discuss equality at work. She pushes back a little, so you practise disagreeing politely with the hedges you learned. Read your lines ('Me') aloud — you are the male learner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jučer smo na poslu razgovarali o ravnopravnosti spolova. Misliš li da su žene i muškarci kod nas stvarno ravnopravni?
lines.en: Yesterday at work we talked about gender equality. Do you think women and men here are really equal?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Čini mi se da još nismo posve ravnopravni, iako je situacija bolja nego prije.
lines.en: It seems to me we're not fully equal yet, although the situation is better than before.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: U čemu vidiš najveći problem?
lines.en: Where do you see the biggest problem?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rekao bih da žene za isti posao katkad dobivaju manju plaću. To mi se čini nepravednim.
lines.en: I'd say that women sometimes get a lower salary for the same job. That seems unfair to me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Slažem se s tobom. A što je s prilikama za napredovanje?
lines.en: I agree with you. And what about opportunities for promotion?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: S jedne strane, ima sve više žena na vodećim položajima. S druge strane, još ih je premalo.
lines.en: On one hand, there are more and more women in leading positions. On the other hand, there are still too few of them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Točno. Možda griješim, ali mislim da su predrasude glavni uzrok.
lines.en: Exactly. Maybe I'm wrong, but I think prejudices are the main cause.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne bih rekao da su jedini uzrok, no sigurno igraju veliku ulogu.
lines.en: I wouldn't say they're the only cause, but they certainly play a big role.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bravo, lijepo si to rekao — 'igraju veliku ulogu'. Zvučiš kao pravi govornik.
lines.en: Well done, you put that nicely — 'they play a big role'. You sound like a real speaker.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala! Trudim se govoriti pristojno čak i kad se ne slažem.
lines.en: Thanks! I try to speak politely even when I disagree.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To je najvažnije. Poštovanje u raspravi vrijedi više od svakog argumenta.
lines.en: That's the most important thing. Respect in a discussion is worth more than any argument.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Potpuno se slažem. Bez poštovanja nema ni prave rasprave.
lines.en: I completely agree. Without respect there's no real discussion either.

212.title: Kako složiti mišljenje — struktura i vezanje teksta
212.intro: Your strongest opinion text yet needs a clear skeleton: an opening, a thesis, ordered arguments with examples, one counter-move, and a conclusion. These connectors are the joints that hold the twelve sentences together. Learn where each one belongs.

items.en: I think that… / I consider that…
items.note: The thesis opener. Always followed by a full clause: Mislim da svi trebaju imati jednaka prava.
Introduces your first argument. Continue with: Kao drugo… / Nadalje… for the next ones.
Adds another argument in the same direction. Similar: Osim toga = besides that.
Piles on support. osim + genitive elsewhere: osim mene = except me.
Introduces a concrete example that backs the argument. Keeps the text from being abstract.
The counter-move: signals a contrast or a concession. Međutim usually opens a sentence.
Balances a point you made earlier; pairs with s jedne strane.
Draws a consequence. Do not confuse zbog (+ noun in G) with zato (a connector).
Opens the closing sentence. Sum up, don't add new arguments.
A strong closing structure: Ravnopravnost nije samo pravo, nego i odgovornost.

212.title: Ogledni tekst: Jesu li svi u društvu ravnopravni?
212.intro: Here is a model twelve-sentence opinion text. Read each sentence with its note: it shows you exactly where the checklist items live — one passive participle, one se-construction, one conditional, one collective numeral, and several connectors.

items.hr: Pitanje ravnopravnosti jedno je od najvažnijih pitanja današnjeg društva.
items.en: The question of equality is one of the most important questions of today's society.
items.note: Opening sentence — sets the topic without yet giving your view.

items.hr: Mislim da svi ljudi trebaju imati jednaka prava, bez obzira na spol, dob ili podrijetlo.
items.en: I think all people should have equal rights, regardless of sex, age or origin.
items.note: The thesis: Mislim da + clause; bez obzira na + accusative.

items.hr: Kao prvo, jednakost pred zakonom temelj je svakoga pravednog društva.
items.en: Firstly, equality before the law is the foundation of every just society.
items.note: First argument, opened by the connector Kao prvo.

items.hr: Nadalje, jednake prilike u obrazovanju omogućuju svakom djetetu bolju budućnost.
items.en: Furthermore, equal opportunities in education give every child a better future.
items.note: Second argument, connector Nadalje; omogućiti + dative (svakom djetetu).

items.hr: Zakonom je zajamčena ravnopravnost, ali stvarnost je često drukčija.
items.en: Equality is guaranteed by law, but reality is often different.
items.note: CHECKLIST — passive participle: zajamčena (from zajamčiti), agreeing with ravnopravnost (f.).

items.hr: Na primjer, žene i danas za isti posao ponekad dobivaju manju plaću.
items.en: For example, even today women sometimes receive a lower salary for the same work.
items.note: A concrete example, opened by Na primjer.

items.hr: Kad bismo svi imali jednake mogućnosti, društvo bi bilo pravednije.
items.en: If we all had equal opportunities, society would be fairer.
items.note: CHECKLIST — conditional: Kad bismo… imali + društvo bi bilo (potential condition).

items.hr: Diskriminacija se, nažalost, još uvijek događa u mnogim područjima.
items.en: Discrimination, unfortunately, still happens in many areas.
items.note: CHECKLIST — se-construction: događa se (reflexive-passive, subject diskriminacija).

items.hr: Dvoje mojih prijatelja doživjelo je nepravdu na poslu zbog svojih godina.
items.en: Two of my friends experienced injustice at work because of their age.
items.note: CHECKLIST — collective numeral: dvoje + genitive plural prijatelja, verb in neuter sg doživjelo je.

items.hr: Međutim, vjerujem da se stvari polako mijenjaju nabolje.
items.en: However, I believe that things are slowly changing for the better.
items.note: The counter-move / concession, opened by Međutim.

items.hr: Zaključno, ravnopravnost nije samo pravo, nego i odgovornost svih nas.
items.en: In conclusion, equality is not only a right, but also a responsibility of us all.
items.note: The closing, using nije samo…, nego i… — a strong final structure.

212.title: Poveznice i struktura
212.intro: Check that you can place the connectors correctly and produce each checklist item on demand. The difficulty climbs from the thesis opener to the trickiest se-construction.

questions.prompt: Thesis opener: 'I think that…' — ______ da svi trebamo jednaka prava.
questions.explanation: Mislim da… = I think that… The 1st sg present is Mislim; misao is the noun 'thought'.

questions.prompt: First argument: '______, jednakost je temelj društva.'
questions.explanation: Kao prvo = firstly, opens the first argument. Na kraju/Zaključno close; Međutim contrasts.

questions.prompt: Which connector signals a contrast ('however')?
questions.explanation: Međutim = however (contrast). Osim toga/Nadalje add support; Naime explains ('namely').

questions.prompt: Passive participle: 'Equality is guaranteed by law.' — Zakonom je ______ ravnopravnost. (zajamčiti → f.)
questions.explanation: The passive participle agrees with ravnopravnost (feminine): zajamčena. Zakonom is the instrumental of means.

questions.prompt: Conditional pair: 'Kad bismo imali jednake mogućnosti, društvo ______ pravednije.'
questions.explanation: A potential condition uses kondicional in both clauses: Kad bismo imali…, društvo bi bilo… (bi + participle bilo).

questions.prompt: Collective numeral: '______ mojih prijatelja doživjelo je nepravdu.'
questions.explanation: A general/mixed group takes the collective numeral dvoje + genitive plural prijatelja, with a neuter singular verb (doživjelo je).

questions.prompt: Build the conclusion: 'Equality is not only a right but also a responsibility.'
questions.answer: Ravnopravnost nije samo pravo nego i odgovornost
questions.explanation: The structure nije samo…, nego i… frames the two parts: not only a right, but also a responsibility.

questions.prompt: se-construction: 'Discrimination still happens.' — Diskriminacija se još uvijek ______.
questions.explanation: Reflexive-passive with a singular subject: događa se (3rd sg). događaju se would need a plural subject.

questions.prompt: Closing connector, one word: '______, ravnopravnost je odgovornost svih nas.' (in conclusion)
questions.explanation: Zaključno = in conclusion, opens the closing sentence. Na kraju is an accepted alternative in speech.

213.title: Pregled 30. tjedna — ravnopravnost, glagolske obitelji, stav
213.intro: Week 30 on one page. Reread these consolidated sentences aloud: the equality vocabulary, the prefixed verb families (staviti, pustiti, dati, pisati), the dative of feeling and the conditional — everything you'll reach for in today's long conversation.

items.hr: Svatko treba dobiti priliku da pokaže što zna.
items.en: Everyone should get a chance to show what they know.
items.note: dati family: dobiti = to get/receive; da + present (da pokaže) for purpose.

items.hr: Ne smijemo dopustiti diskriminaciju na radnome mjestu.
items.en: We must not allow discrimination in the workplace.
items.note: pustiti family: dopustiti = to allow. ne smjeti + infinitive = must not.

items.hr: Poslodavac je postavio jasna pravila za sve zaposlenike.
items.en: The employer set clear rules for all employees.
items.note: staviti family: postaviti = to set/establish. Male subject → postavio.

items.hr: Propustili smo dobru priliku jer nismo reagirali na vrijeme.
items.en: We missed a good opportunity because we didn't react in time.
items.note: pustiti family: propustiti = to miss (an opportunity). jer + clause = because.

items.hr: Napisao sam prijedlog i predao ga upravi.
items.en: I wrote a proposal and submitted it to management.
items.note: pisati family: napisati; dati family: predati = to hand over/submit (+ dative upravi).

items.hr: Sviđa mi se što se stvari polako mijenjaju.
items.en: I like that things are slowly changing.
items.note: Dative experiencer sviđa mi se + se-construction mijenjaju se.

items.hr: Dvoje kolega složilo se s mojim prijedlogom.
items.en: Two colleagues agreed with my proposal.
items.note: Collective dvoje + G pl kolega; složiti se s + instrumental; neuter sg verb složilo se.

items.hr: Kad bismo se svi trudili, radna bi atmosfera bila bolja.
items.en: If we all made an effort, the work atmosphere would be better.
items.note: Potential condition: Kad bismo… + atmosfera bi bila (kondicional in both clauses).

items.hr: Ravnopravnost je zajamčena zakonom, ali treba je i živjeti.
items.en: Equality is guaranteed by law, but it also needs to be lived.
items.note: Passive participle zajamčena + impersonal treba + infinitive.

213.title: Miješani test 30. tjedna
213.intro: One pass across the week: prefixed verb families, the dative of feeling, collective numerals, conditionals and the passive participle. Read each sentence fully before choosing.

questions.prompt: 'We mustn't allow discrimination.' — Ne smijemo ______ diskriminaciju.
questions.explanation: dopustiti = to allow/permit. propustiti = to miss; spustiti = to lower; pustiti = to let go.

questions.prompt: staviti family: 'The employer set clear rules.' — Poslodavac je ______ jasna pravila. (postaviti → m.)
questions.explanation: postaviti = to set/establish; male subject → postavio. postaviti pravila = to lay down rules.

questions.prompt: 'We missed a good opportunity.' — ______ smo dobru priliku.
questions.explanation: propustiti priliku = to miss an opportunity. The pro- prefix carries the sense of letting something pass by.

questions.prompt: Dative of feeling: 'I like that things are changing.' — ______ mi se što se stvari mijenjaju.
questions.explanation: The subject is the što-clause, so the verb is 3rd sg: Sviđa mi se… .

questions.prompt: dati family: 'Everyone should get a chance.' — Svatko treba ______ priliku. (dobiti → infinitive)
questions.explanation: trebati + infinitive; dobiti priliku = to get a chance. dobiti is the perfective 'to receive'.

questions.prompt: Collective numeral: '______ kolega složilo se s prijedlogom.'
questions.explanation: dvoje + genitive plural kolega, with a neuter singular verb složilo se — the standard mixed/general two.

questions.prompt: Build: 'If I had the chance, I would change a lot.'
questions.answer: Da imam priliku promijenio bih mnogo toga
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: Da + present (imam) in the if-clause, kondicional (promijenio bih) in the main clause. mnogo toga = a lot (of it).

questions.prompt: Passive participle: 'Equality is guaranteed by law.' — Ravnopravnost je ______ zakonom.
questions.explanation: The participle agrees with ravnopravnost (feminine sg): zajamčena. zakonom = instrumental of means.

questions.prompt: pisati / dati families: 'I wrote a proposal and submitted it to management.' — Napisao sam prijedlog i ______ ga upravi.
questions.explanation: predati = to hand over / submit to an authority (predati upravi). prodati = to sell; izdati = to publish/betray; zadati = to assign.

213.title: Duži razgovor: društvo, poštenje i posao
213.intro: Your long speaking session for the week. You tell your wife about a proposal you made at work and you range over society, fairness and change — deliberately using the prefixed verb families you've trained. Read your lines ('Me') aloud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Danas si imao sastanak na poslu, zar ne? Kako je prošlo?
lines.en: You had a meeting at work today, didn't you? How did it go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobro. Napisao sam prijedlog o ravnopravnijim uvjetima rada i predao ga upravi.
lines.en: Well. I wrote a proposal about fairer working conditions and submitted it to management.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Svaka čast! O čemu se točno radi?
lines.en: Well done! What exactly is it about?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Predložio sam da svi zaposlenici dobiju jednake prilike za napredovanje.
lines.en: I proposed that all employees get equal opportunities for promotion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To je važno. Nadam se da te nisu odbili.
lines.en: That's important. I hope they didn't reject you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nisu. Dvoje kolega odmah se složilo sa mnom, a šef je postavio nekoliko pitanja.
lines.en: They didn't. Two colleagues agreed with me right away, and the boss asked a few questions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A što je s onima koji se ne slažu?
lines.en: And what about those who don't agree?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Uvijek ima takvih, ali ne smijemo dopustiti da strah zaustavi promjene.
lines.en: There are always such people, but we mustn't let fear stop change.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Slažem se. Da svi šute, ništa se ne bi promijenilo.
lines.en: I agree. If everyone stayed silent, nothing would change.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Upravo tako. Kad bismo se svi malo više trudili, atmosfera bi bila puno bolja.
lines.en: Exactly. If we all tried a little harder, the atmosphere would be much better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ponosna sam na tebe. I govoriš sve tečnije — koristiš i kondicional i glagolske obitelji!
lines.en: I'm proud of you. And you speak more and more fluently — you use both the conditional and the verb families!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trudim se. Šteta bi bila propustiti sve što sam ovih mjeseci naučio.
lines.en: I'm trying. It would be a shame to waste everything I've learned these months.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nećeš ništa propustiti. Hajde, zaslužio si odmor večeras.
lines.en: You won't waste anything. Come on, you've earned a rest tonight.

214.title: Hrvatski humor — vicevi i izrazi
214.intro: Understanding a joke in Croatian is real comprehension: you have to catch the punchline in real time, often built on a double meaning. Learn the vocabulary of humour so you can follow a comedy clip and explain one joke to your wife.

items.note: pričati viceve = to tell jokes; G vica, N pl vicevi. Classic characters: Mujo i Haso, plavuša (the blonde).
Šalim se! = I'm joking! šala = a joke/prank; nema šale = no kidding.
Baš je smiješno! = That's really funny! Do not confuse with smiješan (adj., m.) = funny/ridiculous.
Nasmijao me do suza = he made me laugh to tears. Reflexive nasmijati se = to laugh, to burst out laughing.
Nisam skužio poantu = I didn't get the point. skužiti (colloquial) = to grasp/get it.
A quick, clever remark. dosjetljiv = witty, quick-thinking.
Rekao je to s ironijom = he said it ironically. Croatian humour leans heavily on both.
Idiom, literally 'I cracked from laughter'. Female: pukla sam od smijeha.
Colloquial. Samo te zafrkavam = I'm just teasing you. Reflexive: zezamo se = we're kidding around.

items.en: to have a sense of humour
items.note: Ona ima odličan smisao za humor. smisao za + accusative.

214.title: Gramatički pregled I: vid, trpni pridjev, glagolske imenice
214.intro: The first half of the full B1 grammar sweep. Reread each contrast: the imperfective vs perfective pair, the passive participle that turns a verb into an adjective, and the verbal noun that turns it into a thing. Any of these can be an exam gap.

items.note: Cijelu večer sam pisao (process). Napisao sam pismo (finished result). The na- prefix perfectivises.
Čitao sam knjigu (was reading). Pročitao sam cijelu knjigu (read it all through). cijelu → perfective.
Pismo je napisano (n.). The participle agrees like an adjective: napisan tekst, napisana poruka.
From otvoriti / zatvoriti. Vrata su otvorena (neuter pl vrata → otvorena). Trgovina je zatvorena.
From pisati; neuter noun in -nje. Pisanje eseja traži vrijeme = writing an essay takes time.
Verbal nouns are neuter and formed from the passive participle stem + -e: učenje, putovanje.
A verbal noun governs the genitive of its object: učenje jezika, pisanje teksta, čitanje knjige.

items.hr: Nakon dugog putovanja bili smo umorni.
items.en: After the long journey we were tired.
items.note: Verbal noun putovanje declines like any noun: nakon + genitive putovanja.
Passive participle riješen (from riješiti). Note the double meaning of the -je verbal noun rješenje = a solution.

214.title: Humor i gramatika — pregled I
214.intro: A little humour vocabulary and a solid pass over aspect, the passive participle and verbal nouns. The difficulty climbs from a joke word to a participle-agreement trap.

questions.prompt: 'I'm joking!' — ______ se!
questions.explanation: šaliti se → Šalim se = I'm joking. Šaljem is 'I send' (slati); Salim has no diacritic and is wrong.

questions.prompt: 'the punchline of the joke' — ______ vica.
questions.explanation: poanta = the point/punchline (nominative). poantu is accusative; the spelling pointa is not standard.

questions.prompt: Aspect — a finished whole: 'Yesterday I read the whole book.' — Jučer sam ______ cijelu knjigu.
questions.explanation: cijelu knjigu = a completed whole → perfective pročitati → pročitao. čitao names only the process.

questions.prompt: Verbal noun: 'Learning languages takes time.' — ______ jezika traži vrijeme. (učiti → verbal noun)
questions.explanation: The verbal noun of učiti is učenje (neuter). It governs the genitive of its object: učenje jezika.

questions.prompt: Passive participle: 'The letter is written.' — Pismo je ______.
questions.explanation: The participle agrees with pismo (neuter sg): napisano. napisan is masculine, napisana feminine.

questions.prompt: Aspect — process: 'I was writing all evening.' — Cijelu večer sam ______.
questions.explanation: 'all evening' describes a process with no stated end → imperfective pisati → pisao. napisao would mean the writing was completed.

questions.prompt: Build the idiom: 'I burst out laughing at that joke.'
questions.answer: Pukao sam od smijeha na taj vic
questions.explanation: pukao sam od smijeha = I burst out laughing (od + genitive smijeha); na taj vic = at that joke (na + accusative).

questions.prompt: Verbal noun + genitive object: 'the writing of the text' — pisanje ______.
questions.explanation: A verbal noun governs the genitive: pisanje teksta. The other cases don't fit an object here.

questions.prompt: Participle agreement: 'The doors are open.' — Vrata su ______.
questions.explanation: vrata is a neuter plural (pluralia tantum), so the participle is otvorena: Vrata su otvorena.

215.title: Gramatički pregled II: dativ nositelja, pogodbe, zbirne imenice, se-konstrukcije
215.intro: The second half of the grammar sweep certifies your B1 core. Reread each sentence aloud and name the rule: the dative of the experiencer, the three conditional types, the collective nouns, and the se-constructions. If any one feels shaky, that's tomorrow's exam-prep target.

items.note: Dative experiencer: neuter adverb + dative. Never 'Ja sam hladan' for feeling cold.
The verb agrees with the subject prijatelji (plural): nedostaju. Nedostaje mi more (sg.).

items.hr: Ako budeš vježbao, položit ćeš ispit.
items.en: If you practise, you'll pass the exam.
items.note: Real condition: ako + futur II (budeš vježbao) → futur I (položit ćeš) in the main clause.

items.hr: Kad bih mogao, preselio bih se na more.
items.en: If I could, I'd move to the seaside.
items.note: Potential condition: kad + kondicional (kad bih mogao) → kondicional (preselio bih se).

items.hr: Da imam više vremena, više bih čitao.
items.en: If I had more time, I'd read more.
items.note: Unreal condition: da + present in the if-clause signals the condition is not met.
Collective numerals + genitive plural: dvoje djece, troje ljudi. Used for mixed or general groups.
Impersonal se + 3rd sg: the general 'one'. A subjectless sign sentence.
Reflexive-passive: the subject kruh is 3rd sg, so peče se agrees with it.
Collective noun lišće (from list) is neuter singular: lišće pada. Also cvijeće = flowers, granje = branches.
The collective braća takes a neuter plural participle: stigla. Similarly gospoda, djeca.

215.title: Miješani test — pregled II
215.intro: The certifying pass: the dative of feeling, all three conditionals, collective nouns and se-constructions. Read carefully — the endings and the agreement are where points are won or lost.

questions.prompt: 'I'm cold.' — ______ mi je.
questions.explanation: The frame uses the neuter adverb + dative: Hladno mi je. 'Ja sam hladan' would describe you as cold to the touch.

questions.prompt: Agreement: 'I miss my friends.' — ______ mi prijatelji.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with the plural subject prijatelji: Nedostaju mi prijatelji. Singular: Nedostaje mi more.

questions.prompt: Real condition (futur II): 'If you practise, you'll pass.' — Ako ______ vježbao, položit ćeš. (biti → 2nd sg)
questions.explanation: Futur II = budem/budeš… + participle: Ako budeš vježbao… The main clause then takes futur I (položit ćeš).

questions.prompt: Collective numeral: 'three people' — ______ ljudi.
questions.explanation: A general/mixed group takes troje + genitive plural: troje ljudi. trojica is 'three men' specifically.

questions.prompt: Reflexive-passive: 'Bread is baked every morning.' — Kruh se ______ svako jutro.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with the singular subject kruh: peče se. A plural subject (kolači) would give peku se.

questions.prompt: Potential condition: 'If I could, I'd move to the seaside.' — Kad bih mogao, ______ se na more.
questions.explanation: A potential condition keeps kondicional in both clauses: Kad bih mogao… preselio bih se.

questions.prompt: Build the unreal condition: 'If I had time, I'd read more.'
questions.answer: Da imam vremena više bih čitao
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: Da + present (imam vremena) → kondicional (više bih čitao). da + indicative signals the condition is not fulfilled.

questions.prompt: Collective noun: 'The leaves are falling.' — ______ pada.
questions.explanation: The collective of list is lišće (neuter singular), so the verb is singular: Lišće pada. The spelling 'listje' is wrong.

questions.prompt: Collective agreement: 'The brothers have arrived.' — Braća su ______.
questions.explanation: The collective braća takes a neuter plural participle: Braća su stigla. Same pattern as djeca su došla.

215.title: Slavlje: Moj put do hrvatskoga
215.intro: The Phase 4 milestone. You and your wife celebrate finishing the B1 core and look back over your journey. Tomorrow the exam-prep phase begins. Read your lines ('Me') aloud — you are the male learner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Danas je poseban dan — završio si cijelu B1 razinu! Kako se osjećaš?
lines.en: Today is a special day — you've finished the whole B1 level! How do you feel?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ponosno i pomalo nevjerojatno. Prije godinu dana jedva sam sastavljao rečenice.
lines.en: Proud and a little incredulous. A year ago I could barely put sentences together.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sjećaš se svoje prve snimke? Da je sad poslušaš, ne bi vjerovao koliko si napredovao.
lines.en: Do you remember your first recording? If you listened to it now, you wouldn't believe how much you've progressed.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znam. Da imam vremena, danas bih je ponovno preslušao i usporedio s novom.
lines.en: I know. If I had time, I'd listen to it again today and compare it with a new one.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Što ti je bilo najteže na tom putu?
lines.en: What was the hardest part of the journey for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Padeži, bez sumnje. Dugo mi nije bilo jasno kada dolazi genitiv, a kada lokativ.
lines.en: The cases, no doubt. For a long time it wasn't clear to me when the genitive comes and when the locative.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A sad? Zvučiš gotovo kao domaći govornik.
lines.en: And now? You sound almost like a native speaker.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala! Najviše mi je pomoglo to što svaki dan razgovaram s tobom.
lines.en: Thanks! What helped me most is talking with you every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I bit će još bolje. Sutra počinje priprema za ispit, zar ne?
lines.en: And it'll get even better. Tomorrow the exam prep begins, right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da. Malo sam nervozan, ali osjećam da sam spreman za sljedeći korak.
lines.en: Yes. I'm a little nervous, but I feel I'm ready for the next step.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bit ćeš odličan. Večeras slavimo, a od sutra učimo format ispita!
lines.en: You'll be great. Tonight we celebrate, and from tomorrow we learn the exam format!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dogovoreno. Bez tebe ništa od ovoga ne bih uspio.
lines.en: Agreed. Without you I wouldn't have managed any of this.

216.title: Format ispita — pet dijelova i pravilo prolaza
216.intro: Twenty-five days to exam-ready, and the first rule is: no surprises. The official exam (Ispit iz poznavanja hrvatskoga jezika, NN 100/2021) has five sections in a fixed order and a strict pass rule. Learn the shape by heart so that on the day you spend energy on answers, not on figuring out the format.

items.note: Each recording is played twice; the text stays hidden. Short sentences and one or two longer announcements, with open answers, MCQ and DA/NE (true/false) tasks.
One or more texts (often a biography or an advert), with open questions and a word-bank gap-fill. All answers come FROM the text.
Form-choice tasks: pick the correct grammatical form from four options. Genitive plural, futur I spelling, conditional, prepositions, comparatives.
Two short writing tasks (e.g. describe your day, write a CV or a message). Marked pass/fail against a rubric.
A conversation with the examiner: introduce yourself, describe, compare. Marked pass/fail.
At least 60% in each of listening, reading and grammar, AND a pass in writing and speaking. Every section must be passed separately.

items.en: The listening text is heard twice.
items.note: Use the first play for the gist and the second to verify details — the exam's built-in strategy.
A missing č, ć, š, ž or đ is a spelling error that costs points in the written and gap-fill tasks. Proofread every word.

216.title: Slušanje A: kratke rečenice i najave
216.intro: The first listening task, exactly in the exam format: short sentences and announcements, each heard with the text hidden. Catch the one fact that answers the question — traffic reports, times, numbers and DA/NE announcements. Play each item, then answer.

questions.prompt: Koliko se čeka na graničnom prijelazu?
questions.audioText: Na graničnom prijelazu Bregana putnička vozila na izlazu iz zemlje čekaju oko trideset minuta.
questions.explanation: The recording says vehicles wait 'oko trideset minuta' (about 30 minutes) at the Bregana crossing.

questions.prompt: Zašto je zatvorena autocesta kod Karlovca?
questions.audioText: Zbog prometne nesreće privremeno je zatvorena autocesta A1 kod Karlovca. Vozače molimo za strpljenje.
questions.explanation: 'Zbog prometne nesreće' = because of a traffic accident.

questions.prompt: Koji broj zovete ako vam se pokvari auto? (upišite broj)
questions.audioText: Ako vam se na putu pokvari automobil, nazovite Hrvatski autoklub na broj tisuću devetsto osamdeset sedam.
questions.explanation: 1987 is the Croatian Auto Club (HAK) roadside-assistance number.

questions.prompt: U koliko sati počinje predstava?
questions.audioText: Poštovani posjetitelji, večerašnja kazališna predstava počinje u devetnaest i trideset. Molimo vas da zauzmete svoja mjesta.
questions.explanation: 'u devetnaest i trideset' = at 19:30 (half past seven in the evening).

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Trgovina radi nedjeljom.
questions.audioText: Obavještavamo cijenjene kupce da naša trgovina radi od ponedjeljka do subote, a nedjeljom ne radi.
questions.explanation: The shop works Monday to Saturday, and 'nedjeljom ne radi' — it is closed on Sundays.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Let za Frankfurt kasni.
questions.audioText: Poštovani putnici, let za Frankfurt predviđen u petnaest sati kasni zbog lošega vremena. Novo vrijeme polijetanja je šesnaest i trideset.
questions.explanation: The flight 'kasni zbog lošega vremena' and the new departure is 16:30 instead of 15:00 — so yes, it is delayed.

questions.prompt: Od koliko do koliko sati radi pošta radnim danom? (npr. 'od 8 do 19')
questions.audioText: Pošta u središtu grada radi radnim danom od osam do devetnaest sati, a subotom do trinaest sati.
questions.explanation: 'radnim danom od osam do devetnaest sati' = on weekdays from 8 to 19.

217.title: Slušanje B: strategija za tvrdnje DA/NE
217.intro: The second listening task is one longer text — usually an event announcement — heard twice, with six DA/NE (true/false) statements. The single most valuable habit: read all the statements BEFORE the audio starts, so you know what to listen for. Learn the strategy and the announcement vocabulary.

items.en: Read all the statements before listening.
items.note: The statements tell you which facts matter. Underline names, numbers, times and any negation.
Don't panic if you miss a detail; get the overall picture of what the announcement is about.
Now confirm each DA/NE one by one against what you hear.

items.hr: Pazi na negacije: ne, nije, nikad.
items.note: A single 'ne' flips a statement from DA to NE. 'Ulaz nije besplatan' means it is NOT free.

items.en: to take place, to be held
items.note: Koncert će se održati u dvorani = the concert will be held in the hall. Reflexive; often in the future.
kulturno događanje = a cultural event. Common in announcements of festivals and fairs.
ulaz slobodan = free admission. Watch: is entry free (besplatan) or do you need a ticket (ulaznica)?
Molimo da se prijavite na broj… = please register at the number… A frequent 'catch' in the statements.

217.title: Slušanje B: najava proljetnoga sajma knjige
217.intro: One longer announcement, exactly in the exam format. Read all six DA/NE statements first, then play the announcement (it plays twice, text hidden) and mark each true or false. The final item checks one detail.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Sajam se održava u gradskoj knjižnici.
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici' — it is held in the city library.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Ulaz na sajam treba platiti.
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje' — entry is free, so no, you don't pay.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Sajam traje tri dana.
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'od petka do nedjelje' = from Friday to Sunday — three days.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Susret sa spisateljicom održava se u nedjelju.
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: The meeting with the writer is 'u subotu u podne' — on Saturday at noon, not Sunday.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Za radionicu se treba prijaviti unaprijed.
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed' — you must register in advance for the workshop.

questions.prompt: U koliko sati počinje sajam?
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'Sajam počinje u deset sati' = the fair starts at 10 o'clock.

questions.prompt: Tko dolazi u subotu u podne? (dovršite: poznata hrvatska ______)
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom' — a meeting with a famous Croatian (woman) writer. The base form is spisateljica.

218.title: Čitanje A: biografija — Marija Jurić Zagorka
218.intro:
Exam Reading A is a biography of about 150 words followed by open questions. Every answer must come FROM the text — no invention, full sentences, correct case endings. First read the biography below, then learn the reading strategy and the biography vocabulary.

MARIJA JURIĆ ZAGORKA
Marija Jurić Zagorka rodila se 1873. godine u mjestu Negovec pokraj Vrbovca. Bila je prva profesionalna novinarka u Hrvatskoj i jedna od najčitanijih hrvatskih spisateljica. Radila je u novinama Obzor, iako su se mnogi u to doba protivili tome da žena bude novinarka. Pisala je o pravima žena i borila se protiv nepravde. Najpoznatija je po povijesnim romanima kao što su 'Grička vještica' i 'Republikanci', koje su čitali ljudi iz svih društvenih slojeva. Osim toga, pokrenula je i uređivala prve časopise za žene u Hrvatskoj. Cijeli je život živjela skromno, posvećena pisanju i borbi za ravnopravnost. Umrla je u Zagrebu 1957. godine. Danas jedna ulica u Zagrebu nosi njezino ime, a svake se godine dodjeljuje ugledna novinarska nagrada koja se zove po njoj.

items.hr: Strategija: prvo pronađi imena, datume i mjesta.
items.en: Strategy: first locate names, dates and places.
items.note: Scan the text for capitalised names and numbers before you read the questions. That's where most answers hide.
Never add what you happen to know about the person. If it isn't in the text, it isn't the answer.

items.hr: Piši potpune rečenice s točnim padežom.
items.en: Write full sentences with the correct case.
items.note: 'Gdje se rodila?' → 'Rodila se u Negovcu' (locative), not just 'Negovec'.
novinar/novinarka; pisac or spisatelj/spisateljica. Zagorka was the first professional female journalist in Croatia.
rodila se 1873. godine; umrla je 1957. godine. Years take 'godine' in the genitive.
'Grička vještica' is her most famous historical novel. roman → G romana, N pl romani.
pokrenula je časopis = she launched a magazine; uređivati = to edit (a publication).
Nagrada 'Marija Jurić Zagorka' se dodjeljuje novinarima — the prize is awarded to journalists. dodjeljivati se = to be awarded (se-passive).

218.title: Pitanja uz biografiju
218.intro: Answer each question from the Zagorka biography you just read. Locate the fact first, then answer. The difficulty climbs from a place to a two-part fact at the end.

questions.prompt: Gdje se rodila Marija Jurić Zagorka?
questions.explanation: 'rodila se 1873. godine u mjestu Negovec pokraj Vrbovca.' Location → locative: u Negovcu.

questions.prompt: Čime se bavila?
questions.explanation: 'prva profesionalna novinarka u Hrvatskoj i jedna od najčitanijih hrvatskih spisateljica.'

questions.prompt: Koje se godine rodila? (upišite broj)
questions.explanation: 'rodila se 1873. godine.'

questions.prompt: Po čemu je najpoznatija?
questions.explanation: 'Najpoznatija je po povijesnim romanima kao što su Grička vještica i Republikanci.'

questions.prompt: Kako se zove jedan od njezinih romana?
questions.explanation: The text names 'Grička vještica' and 'Republikanci'. The other three are famous Croatian works by other authors.

questions.prompt: Koje je godine umrla? (upišite broj)
questions.explanation: 'Umrla je u Zagrebu 1957. godine.'

questions.prompt: Što danas nosi njezino ime?
options: ulica u Zagrebu i novinarska nagrada
questions.answer: ulica u Zagrebu i novinarska nagrada
questions.explanation: 'jedna ulica u Zagrebu nosi njezino ime, a svake se godine dodjeljuje ugledna novinarska nagrada koja se zove po njoj.'

219.title: Čitanje B: zadatak s riječima iz okvira
219.intro: The densest exam task: a text with gaps, a bank of words underneath, and you must insert each word in its exact case form. It tests vocabulary and grammar at once. The winning method is one step: decide which case the sentence needs BEFORE you pick the word. Diacritics must be perfect.

items.hr: Prvo odredi padež, pa onda oblik.
items.en: First decide the case, then the form.
items.note: Ask: subject (N)? object (A)? 'of' (G)? 'in/on/about' (L)? 'with/by' (I)? The case dictates the ending.
u travnju, u srpnju, u prosincu. The bare travanj, srpanj, prosinac are only nominatives.

items.en: After numbers 5 and up → genitive plural.
items.note: pet dana, deset ljudi, dvadeset godina. The number 'freezes' the noun in the genitive plural.
N pl oči, G pl očiju, D/L/I pl očima. A classic gap-fill trap: svojim očima.

items.en: in the middle / at the start / at the end of + genitive.
items.note: sredinom srpnja = in mid-July; krajem mjeseca = at the end of the month.
More je bilo ljepše nego ikad (comparative). najveće putovanje (superlative, neuter).
N srce, L u srcu. Neuter noun; the location after 'u' takes the locative.

items.en: Check the diacritics in every word.
items.note: srpnja, ljepše, najveće — a missing č, ć, š, ž or đ costs the point even if the case is right.

219.title: Umetni riječ iz okvira
219.intro: A short holiday text with gaps. The word bank is: putovanje, travanj, mjesec, lijep, dan, oko, srpanj, velik, srce. For each gap decide the case first, then choose the correctly formed word. Difficulty climbs to the irregular and superlative forms.

questions.prompt: 'We love summer trips.' — Volimo ljetna ______. (putovanje → A pl)
questions.explanation: Plural direct object → accusative plural of a neuter noun: putovanja.

questions.prompt: 'In April we travelled to Dalmatia.' — U ______ smo putovali u Dalmaciju. (travanj)
questions.explanation: Month as 'when' → locative: u travnju. The 'a' in travanj drops out in the oblique cases.

questions.prompt: 'We spent there five ______.' — Ondje smo proveli pet ______. (dan → G pl)
questions.explanation: After 5 and up the noun stands in the genitive plural: pet dana.

questions.prompt: 'The sea was more beautiful than ever.' — More je bilo ______ nego ikad. (lijep → comparative)
questions.explanation: Comparative agreeing with more (neuter): ljepše. ljepši is masculine; najljepše is the superlative.

questions.prompt: 'We couldn't believe our own eyes.' — Nismo mogli vjerovati svojim ______. (oko → I pl)
questions.explanation: vjerovati + dative, and 'svojim očima' uses the irregular plural očima. očiju is the genitive plural.

questions.prompt: 'We came back in mid-July.' — Vratili smo se sredinom ______. (srpanj)
questions.explanation: sredinom (+ genitive) = in the middle of: sredinom srpnja. srpanj → srpnja (fleeting a).

questions.prompt: 'It was the biggest trip of our life.' — To je bilo ______ putovanje u našem životu. (velik → superlative, n.)
questions.explanation: Superlative agreeing with putovanje (neuter): najveće. najveći is masculine; veće is only the comparative.

questions.prompt: 'We keep those memories in our heart.' — Čuvamo te uspomene u ______. (srce → L)
questions.explanation: Location after 'u' → locative: u srcu. srcem is the instrumental, srca the genitive.

220.title: Gramatika A: izbor točnoga oblika
220.intro: Exam Section III is form choice: four options, one correct grammatical form. The sample patterns repeat every year — genitive plural after quantities, futur I spelling, the conditional bismo, prepositions with their case, and comparative adverbs. Learn each rule with its example and the trap it hides.

items.note: After numbers and quantities the noun goes to the genitive plural: milijuna stanovnika. '5 milijuna stanovnika', 'pola milijuna ljudi'.
Verbs ending in -ći keep the full infinitive before the auxiliary: ići ćemo, doći ću. Only -ti verbs drop the -i: radit ću, pisat ćemo.
For -ti verbs futur I drops the final -i and joins in writing only as two words: radit ću, čitat ćeš, pisat će.

items.hr: ja bih, mi bismo, vi biste, oni bi
items.note: Standard kondicional: bih/bi/bi + bismo/biste/bi. Colloquial 'bi' for all persons is not accepted in the exam.
među stvarima (among several things, instrumental); između dva grada (between two, genitive).
After a preposition you must use the stressed pronoun: o njemu, s njom, za njega. Never the clitic mu, joj, ga.
Comparative ADVERB: ljepše, brže, bolje. 'Ljepše je živjeti na moru nego u gradu.' ljepši is the adjective.
jer introduces a clause with a verb (jer je kasno); zbog takes a noun in the genitive (zbog kiše).

items.en: on Thursdays, in summer, by day
items.note: The instrumental of time expresses a regular pattern: četvrtkom igram tenis = I play tennis on Thursdays.

220.title: Izaberi točan oblik
220.intro: Form choice, exactly like Section III of the official exam: four options, one correct form, diacritics count. The difficulty climbs from the genitive plural after a number to a superlative in context.

questions.prompt: Hrvatska ima oko četiri milijuna ______.
questions.explanation: Numbers above 4 and the word milijun take the genitive plural: milijuna stanovnika.

questions.prompt: ______ zajedno na posao.
questions.explanation: Verbs in -ći keep the full infinitive: ići ćemo. 'Ić ćemo' and 'Ićemo' are non-standard; 'čemo' has the wrong letter.

questions.prompt: Sutra ______ raditi do kasno.
questions.explanation: For -ti verbs futur I drops the -i: radit ću (two words). 'raditi ću' keeps the -i; 'radit ču' has the wrong letter.

questions.prompt: I mi ______ rado došli na utakmicu!
questions.explanation: 1st person plural conditional is bismo. The colloquial all-purpose 'bi' is not accepted; bih is 1st singular.

questions.prompt: Knjiga je ______ ostalim stvarima na polici.
questions.explanation: 'among several things' → među + instrumental (ostalim stvarima). između + genitive is 'between two'.

questions.prompt: Zakasnio sam ______ se autobus pokvario.
questions.explanation: jer introduces a clause with a verb (se autobus pokvario). zbog would need a noun in the genitive (zbog kvara).

questions.prompt: Čuli smo samo lijepo o ______.
questions.explanation: After a preposition the stressed form is required: o njemu. The clitic 'mu' can never follow a preposition.

questions.prompt: ______ je živjeti na moru nego u gradu.
questions.explanation: Comparative adverb: ljepše… nego… = nicer… than…. 'Ljepši' is the adjective; 'nego' signals the comparison.

questions.prompt: ______ uvijek igram tenis.
questions.explanation: The instrumental of time marks a regular pattern: četvrtkom = on Thursdays.

questions.prompt: To je ______ film koji sam ikad gledao.
questions.explanation: 'the best film' → superlative adjective agreeing with film (masculine): najbolji. najbolje is the adverb.
